data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild generates the `.cs` file on disk but the compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile>` item for the generated file after writing it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The problem is clear. The target `GenerateSampleCode` runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is generated but MSBuild doesn't know to include it in compilation.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target, after the file is written. This tells the compiler to include the generated file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>

    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. The compiler's source file list was already finalized.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation. Build and run now succeed.
